normalized_title: City of Melville Local Planning Policy 1.6 - Parking and Access
scope_summary: Sets parking, access and parking-design standards for residential and non-residential development on all land subject to City of Melville Local Planning Scheme No. 6, except where varied by an approved Precinct Structure Plan, Structure Plan or Local Development Plan. Part 1 replaces specified deemed-to-comply provisions of R-Codes Volume 1 (sightlines cl 5.2.5, vehicular access cl 5.3.5, medium-density access cl 3.7) and Acceptable Outcomes of R-Codes Volume 2 (elements 3.8 and 3.9), generally deferring design to AS 2890.1:2004. Part 2 prescribes non-residential car parking ratios (Table 1), motorcycle/scooter ratios (Table 2), bicycle parking (Table 3), reductions for Activity Centres, reciprocal and shared parking, Travel Plans, Parking Management Plans and traffic generation assessment. It prevails over LPP 3.1 Residential Development to the extent of inconsistency.

key_numeric_standards.topic: driveway setback from side lot boundary or street pole (low density)
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: driveways serving R-Codes Vol 1 Part B residential development
key_numeric_standards.clause_ref: modified DTC C5.3A (replacing R-Codes Vol 1 cl 5.3.5)

key_numeric_standards.applies_to: residential driveways (low and medium density)
key_numeric_standards.clause_ref: modified DTC C5.3A and C3.7.3A iv

key_numeric_standards.topic: wall/fence height within 1.5m of vehicle access points (truncation)
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: internal driveways, rights of way, communal streets - residential
R-Codes Vol 1 Part C medium density driveways

key_numeric_standards.topic: driveway maximum width at street boundary (medium density)
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: R-Codes Vol 1 Part C medium density driveways

key_numeric_standards.topic: driveway setback from side lot boundary (medium density)
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: R-Codes Vol 1 Part C medium density driveways

key_numeric_standards.topic: pier in truncation area - maximum width / fence permeability
key_numeric_standards.value: 0.35 m pier; 0.75 m max wall; 80% visually permeable above
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: fencing within sightline truncation areas

key_numeric_standards.topic: car parking ratio - child care premises
key_numeric_standards.value: 1 bay per 10 children plus 0.5 bays per staff member
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: Child Care Premises (non-residential)
Office
Shop / Home Store / Convenience Store / Restricted Premises

key_numeric_standards.topic: car parking ratio - restaurant/cafe/small bar/fast food
key_numeric_standards.value: 1 bay per 10 m2 PFA plus 0.5 bay per staff member
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: Restaurant / Cafe / Small Bar / Lunch Bar / Fast Food Outlet

key_numeric_standards.topic: car parking ratio - medical centre/consulting rooms
key_numeric_standards.value: 3 bays per health consultant plus 0.5 bay per staff member
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: Medical Centre / Consulting Rooms / Veterinary Clinic
non-residential development

key_numeric_standards.value: 2 bays where 15-29 car bays required, scaling to 10 bays at 75-89 car bays
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: developments providing 15+ car bays
all development sites unless Table 3 specifies more

key_numeric_standards.value: required where more than 6 bicycle spaces required; 1 locker per bicycle space; 1 unisex shower minimum
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: non-residential development
development in SPP 4.2 Secondary or District activity centres with approved PMP

key_numeric_standards.value: up to 100% (no overlap in operating times) or 50% (partial overlap)
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: reciprocal parking arrangements
bays designated as shared parking in Secondary/District activity centres

key_numeric_standards.value: up to 2 car bays reduced at 6 bicycle spaces per car bay
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: developments in Secondary/District activity centres

quality_flags: clean extraction, 21 pages; tables (modified DTC provisions, Tables 1-3) flattened to linear text but values remain legible
source document contains typos carried into extraction: 'AS2980.1' where AS2890.1 is intended (several occurrences), 'AS28990.1' in cl 5.5(a), stray punctuation in cl 5.4
adopted OMC 20/05/2025 (UP25/68) following WAPC Statutory Planning Committee approval 26/02/2025; manifest canonical filename says 'Final Version June 2025' which matches the 6/06/2025 print footer
